title: Tapping to Stop Fighting Myself About Sleep
subtitle: A daytime tapping practice for ending the internal conflict between the body and the need for rest
excerpt: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. The script addresses the exhausting pattern of being at war with your own body over sleep — blaming it, fearing nights, monitoring every sensation. The reframe moves toward the body as a partner in rest rather than the source of the problem.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from the Day 29 daytime tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the sense of being at war with your own sleep pattern is strong — when blaming the body, dreading the night before it arrives, or monitoring every sensation has become a habit.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 29 of a 30-day sleep programme. Day 29 theme: end the battle with sleep. The script works with the exhausting pattern of fighting your own body over rest — blaming it for keeping you awake, fearing nights before they arrive, measuring every bad night as evidence the body has failed. The reframe moves toward cooperation: body and mind wanting the same thing and learning how to reach it together.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I've made sleep my enemy, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've feared the nights and blamed my body, I honour that I was just desperate for rest.
Even though I've been at war with this for years, I'm open to making peace now.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I'm tired of fighting myself.
Side of eye: This battle has only made me lose twice.
Under eye: My body is not the problem — it's the partner.
Under nose: I can't heal in conflict.
Chin: I choose peace with myself.
Collarbone: I drop the armour and the blame.
Under arm: I make room for cooperation instead.
Top of head: Healing begins when the war ends.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I trust my body completely.
Side of eye: We're learning how to rest together.
Under eye: I speak kindly to myself now.
Under nose: Sleep is not the enemy — it's the reunion.
Chin: I'm proud of how far I've come.
Collarbone: Peace feels like relief.
Under arm: I'm done fighting and ready to live.
Top of head: I rest as my own ally tonight.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from the Day 29 daytime tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
